articles.title: HAVE YOU CHOSEN THE WRONG PRICING MODEL?
articles.summary: Seat-based, usage-based, or outcome-based: which pricing strategy is right? Most founders pick a model based on what competitors do, not what fits their product.

articles.title: 8 TAKEAWAYS FROM CARTA'S STATE OF SEED REPORT
articles.summary: Seed funding is back to near-peak levels, but the money is significantly harder to touch. Investors are piling more cash into fewer rounds, essentially ignoring everything outside of SF and NY. The most telling stat is headcount: the average seed startup has slimmed down from 10 people to just 6, likely because AI is picking up the slack. However, don't let the tools fool you into going it alone: VCs still fund co-founders at double the rate of solo acts, despite the data showing that 25% of those partnerships end in a breakup by year four.

articles.title: HARDWARE IS A FRUIT
articles.summary: The feedback loop for physical products is speeding up. We used to assume that building hardware meant long timelines and high costs for every mistake. That dynamic is fading. Modern simulation tools and AI assistants now let engineers iterate on physical designs almost as fast as they write code. The barrier to entry is shifting from manufacturing logistics to pure design capability.

articles.title: DRIVING EXTREME CLARITY
articles.summary: At your startup, it's important to recognize that a "messy yes" is often more dangerous than a hard "no." Teams tend to drift into "strategic ambiguity" - nodding along to avoid conflict without actually agreeing on the trade-offs. The fix is to force the friction upfront: make the team stack-rank priorities (no ties allowed) and write down exactly where you disagree.

articles.title: RL IN REAL LIFE: DURABLE MOATS
articles.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has gone from being a promising path towards general intelligence to a taboo phrase over the past year. It has proved highly effective inside AI labs, but outside of the labs, its use cases remain surprisingly underdeveloped. Most efforts appear to be largely experimental and have yet to scratch the surface of the sophisticated techniques used within the frontier labs. This post looks at the intricacies of building effective RL approaches, assesses current use cases, and discusses what founders should consider when commercializing RL-based systems.

articles.title: A FEW THOUGHTS ON CONVICTION
articles.summary: The venture capital industry is full of sheep. Funds blindly follow others without thinking for themselves. They lack all conviction. As a result, they don't blaze new trails - and blazing new trails is the only way to earn outsized returns in a hyper-competitive market as an investor or an operator.

articles.title: WHY STARTUPS DIE
articles.summary: Most startups eventually fail. This is rarely because of competition. Most of the time, their demise is self-inflicted by internal issues. The biggest threats usually come from within the company's own walls, not from the outside world.

articles.title: IN 2025, I THOUGHT A LOT ABOUT THE THINGS I WANT TO SAY TO MY BOSS
articles.summary: Leadership can't fake that they care because people can feel it.

articles.summary: It turns out the real money in AI isn't going to chatbots or marketing copy - it's almost entirely still going to code.
